description: A shaped metal instrument for opening locks; to unlock or provide access. 'Kira' names what opens—the specific shape that grants passage.
sound_symbolism: The word clicks open. 'Ki' turns with precise mechanism, lock engaging. Then 'ra' reaches through to access, door yielding.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo kira' (the key). As verb: 'mi kira' (I key/unlock). Both physical keys and metaphorical keys to understanding.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Keys to understanding—right view, right effort, right practice unlock insight.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Access through permission—keys represent trust, the granting of entry to protected spaces.
